plan_metadata.name: group  debug in teams group remote

steps.description: Press the F1 key in Visual Studio Code to open the command palette.
Type 'Debug: Select and Start Debugging' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input box at the top of the window.
Press the Enter key to select the "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette.

Type the text "debug view remote app in teams(chrome)" into the VS Code command palette input box to select the "View Remote App in Teams (Chrome)" command in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.
Click the "View Remote App in Teams (chrome)" option in the Visual Studio Code command palette to initiate debugging or viewing a remote app in Microsoft Teams using Chrome.
Click on the "Password" input field in the Microsoft login window to activate it for entering the password.
Type  ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_PASSWORD}} into the password input field on the Microsoft sign-in page for user  ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}.
Click the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft login page after entering the password for the account in the browser.
Click the "Yes" button on the Microsoft sign-in prompt to confirm staying signed in for account  ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}.
Click the “Add” button within the app details  in Microsoft Teams (web interface), indicated by a blue button below the app name and publisher.

Click the "Search for a channel or chat" input box in the app selection popup within the Microsoft Teams web interface. The red crosshair precisely highlights the text field under "Or choose a place to use the app" in the overlay dialog.
Click the "General" channel option (labeled ATK) in the "Choose where to get started" dialog within Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the purple button with the English flag icon at the bottom of the "Choose where to get started" popup in Microsoft Teams web interface.
